avalancheActivity.highlights: At intermediate and high altitudes a considerable avalanche danger will persist.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and a moderate wind, mostly shallow wind slabs formed. Wind slabs can be released, even by a single winter sport participant. This applies in particular on steep slopes, as well as in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain also at intermediate altitudes.
Avalanches can be released in deeper layers in particular on steep shady slopes.
The avalanche prone locations are sometimes covered with new snow difficult to recognise.

Meticulous route selection is required. Maintaining distances between individuals and one-at-a-time descents are recommended.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 15 to 20 cm of snow, and up to 30 cm in some localities, has fallen since Thursday. As a consequence of new snow and a sometimes moderate wind from westerly directions, further wind slabs formed in particular above the tree line. The new snow and wind slabs are lying on soft layers on wind-protected shady slopes.

Intermediate and high altitudes: Isolated avalanche prone weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack in particular on very steep shady slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: As a consequence of new snow and wind a considerable danger of dry avalanches will be encountered over a wide area. This applies in particular above the tree line.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and a moderate wind, further wind slabs formed. The fresh snow and in particular the sometimes large wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant in all aspects and generally above the tree line. This applies in particular on steep slopes, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls.
The avalanches can be released in the new snow and wind slab layers and reach large size.

Careful route selection and spacing between individuals are recommended. Backcountry touring calls for great caution and restraint.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 20 to 30 cm of snow fell in the last two days above approximately 1200 m. As a consequence of snowfall and the moderate westerly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations formed. Over a wide area new snow and wind slabs are lying on soft layers.

Above approximately 1500 m a lot of snow is lying.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Individual avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular in steep terrain at intermediate and high altitudes.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The fresh and somewhat older wind slabs represent the main danger. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas and on steep, little used slopes. The avalanches are medium-sized and can mostly only be released by large loads.
In very isolated cases avalanches can also be triggered in the old snowpack and reach large size.

Maintaining distances between individuals and one-at-a-time descents are recommended.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Some snow fell on Thursday. This applies in particular in particular above approximately 1000 m.
The new snow and wind slabs are lying on soft layers in particular on wind-protected shady slopes.

Intermediate and high altitudes: Isolated avalanche prone weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack in particular on very steep shady slopes.
